@@ -0,0 +1,212 @@
+# BAZ Marketing Site — Competitive Dominance Plan
+
+**Target:** `baz-marketing-site` (repo: `baz-marketing-site`, remote: `origin`)  
+**North Star:** Own every high-intent "marketing agency [city/vertical]" query in MENA, EU, and US; convert at >4% with a site that loads faster and proves more trust than every top-10 competitor.  
+**Scope:** Implementation-ready execution plan. Code changes are marked for an implementation-capable agent.
+
+---
+
+## Current State (from codebase audit)
+
+- **Stack:** Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind, Supabase, Vercel.
+- **Strengths:** Dynamic sitemap, JSON-LD (Organization/WebSite/ProfessionalService/FAQ/Article), sharp B&W design system, `vs-others` comparison page, 18-service catalog, case-study and insights routings.
+- **Gaps blocking domination:**
+  - Security/auth gaps (middleware bypass, missing guards on `/dashboard`, `/admin/monitors`, etc.) — credibility risk.
+  - In-memory rate limiter ineffective on Vercel; dual lead stores (SQLite vs JSONL) cause data divergence.
+  - Dead weight: `motion` in `package.json` unused; stale inspector lists non-existent routes.
+  - Trust content is partly composite: case studies have `placeholder` flags; testimonials are placeholders.
+  - Hardcoded hex colors in components violate the B&W token rule.
+  - No local SEO pages (`/industries/[slug]` exists but lacks city/regional schema), no FAQ-only landing pages, no rich-result breadcrumbs everywhere.
+  - Performance: no `next/font` preload strategy review, no `fetchpriority` on hero images, no explicit `Cache-Control` on public JSON content.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1)
+
+*Win condition: Site passes a fresh security/quality audit; Core Web Vitals hit "Good" on mobile.*
+
+1. **Security hardening (Critical/High)**  
+   - `middleware.ts`: validate `baz_session` via `readSessionFromCookies()` + `user.role` before `NextResponse.next()`.  
+   - Add server-side auth guards to `/admin/monitors`, `/admin/analytics`, `/admin/integrations`, `/admin/canva`, `/dashboard`, `/console`.  
+   - Fix Supabase RLS: replace `USING (true)` with role-scoped policies or remove Supabase anonymous access from protected tables.  
+   - `/api/auth/me`: return real role from local `users` table, not hardcoded `"member"`.
+
+2. **Data layer cleanup (High)**  
+   - Remove `data/leads.jsonl` dependency; migrate `/dashboard` to the SQLite/Supabase canonical `getDb()` path.  
+   - Ensure `/api/leads` is the single source of truth for all lead storage.
+
+3. **Rate limiting (High)**  
+   - Replace in-memory `Map` in `lib/rate-limit.ts` with **Vercel KV** (or Upstash Redis).  
+   - Key buckets on `user.id` for authenticated routes; fall back to trusted `x-forwarded-for` or Vercel `request.ip` for anonymous routes.
+
+4. **Dead code & inspector accuracy (Medium)**  
+   - Remove `motion` from `package.json`.  
+   - Update `scripts/quality-inspect.mjs` route lists to match the actual app tree.  
+   - Add GET handler to `/api/score` or document as POST-only.
+
+5. **Design system compliance (Medium)**  
+   - Replace hardcoded hex colors in `AnalyticsTools.tsx`, `app/api/auth/register/route.ts`, etc., with semantic Tailwind tokens (`text-brand`, `bg-background`, `bg-accent`).  
+   - Fix `layout.tsx` CSS import order so `color-layer.css` is literally last, per DESIGN-SYSTEM.md.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 2: Trust & Authority (Week 2–3)
+
+*Win condition: Every major page contains named, metric-backed proof; no live placeholder remains.*
+
+1. **Case studies — replace composites with signed-client material**  
+   - Keep structure (Problem / Strategy / Result / Duration / Testimonial) but swap names, metrics, and cover images for real signed-off cases.  
+   - Add `schema.org/Review` JSON-LD to each case-study page (`app/case-studies/[slug]/page.tsx`).  
+   - Add internal links from each case study back to the specific service category and to `/vs-others`.
+
+2. **Testimonials — evict placeholders**  
+   - `content/testimonials.ts`: replace 9 `placeholder: true` entries with real client quotes, names, roles, and headshots (or initials avatars).  
+   - Render a `Review` schema block on the `/testimonials` aggregator (or homepage section).
+
+3. **Proof numbers — tighten and link**  
+   - Homepage `ProofNumbers` section: link each stat to the exact case study or service page that proves it.  
+   - Add a `/proof` landing page collecting every metric in one place with explicit date ranges (`Q1 2025`) to reduce skepticism.
+
+4. **Social proof widgets**  
+   - Add a live client counter backed by the canonical `clients` table to the homepage and `/about`.  
+   - Add a "Featured in / trusted by" logo marquee (`/brandbook` assets already exist; wire them into `LogoMarquee`).
+
+---
+
+## Phase 3: SEO Offensive (Week 3–4)
+
+*Win condition: Top-3 rankings for 20+ high-intent service + industry keywords; organic traffic up 40% in 90 days.*
+
+1. **Service page depth**  
+   - Each `/services/[slug]` page already has pillar/process/FAQ/proof blocks. Add:  
+     - `FAQPage` schema (using existing `faqLd()` helper).  
+     - Schema `Service` type blocks (with `areaServed`, `offers`).  
+     - Breadcrumb schema (`breadcrumbLd()`).  
+     - "Start a project" contact form tailored to the service.
+
+2. **Industry pages (`/industries/[slug]`)**  
+   - Expand from generic landing pages to location + vertical stacks (e.g., `industries/fintech`, `industries/fintech/seo`, `industries/dubai`).  
+   - Add `LocalBusiness` / `ProfessionalService` schema with `areaServed` city/region.  
+   - Target long-tail queries: "SEO agency for fintech in Dubai", "CRO agency for SaaS in London".
+
+3. **Insights expansion**  
+   - Increase `posts.ts` from 5 to 20 articles in 90 days. Target:  
+     - 3 comparison pieces (`vs` competitors) linked from `/vs-others`.  
+     - 5 "how-we-do-it" methodology posts linked from `/methodology`.  
+     - 3 local-perspective posts targeting MENA, EU, US.  
+   - Add `Article` schema (standardize `author` with `Person` + `sameAs`).  
+   - Add RSS feed (`/feed.xml` exists; verify it emits full content and includes new posts).
+
+4. **Internal linking & crawl budget**  
+   - Add related-article and related-service modules on every detail page.  
+   - Ensure `/vs-others` is linked from the homepage and every service page footer.  
+   - Fix `color-layer.css` order comment in `layout.tsx` to avoid confusion.
+
+5. **Images & OG**  
+   - Add `alt` text to every `<Image>` (currently missing/weak).  
+   - Generate service-specific OG images (`/og/services/[slug]`) or update `/og` to accept service title/subtitle so social shares don't all show the same generic image.  
+   - Add `og:image:alt` and `og:locale`.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 4: Speed & UX (Week 4–5)
+
+*Win condition: LCP < 1.5s (mobile), CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms.*
+
+1. **Core Web Vitals**  
+   - `HomePage` is static (good). Audit every dynamic `[slug]` page for `loading.tsx` + `suspense` boundaries to prevent layout shift.  
+   - Add `fetchpriority="high"` to the hero `<Image>` in `Hero.tsx`.  
+   - Preload critical fonts (`Inter`, `Outfit`, `Poppins`, `JetBrains Mono`) in `layout.tsx` with `<link rel="preload">` for the hero section.
+
+2. **Caching strategy**  
+   - Add explicit `Cache-Control` headers in `vercel.json` for `/services`, `/case-studies`, `/insights`, `/industries` (`public, s-maxage=600, stale-while-revalidate=3600`).  
+   - Ensure `/api/services` and `/api/health` remain no-store (already present).
+
+3. **Accessibility**  
+   - Fix `Modal` role/aria-modal/focus trap (per AUDIT §8.1).  
+   - Add `alt` text to all decorative SVGs or mark `aria-hidden="true"`.  
+   - Verify focus-visible outlines on interactive elements (buttons, links) match the B&W system.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 5: Conversion Architecture (Week 5–6)
+
+*Win condition: Contact/booking conversion rate > 4%; bounce rate < 45%.*
+
+1. **CTA placement and copy**  
+   - Audit every section for a single primary CTA (not multiple competing buttons).  
+   - A/B test two CTAs in the hero: "Get your 90-day forecast" vs "Book a growth call".  
+   - Add context-aware CTAs: service pages → "Start a [service] engagement"; case-study pages → "Get similar results"; insights → "Discuss this for your brand".
+
+2. **Booking integration**  
+   - Replace the `mailto:` fallback in `site.ts` with a real booking URL (Cal.com or equivalent).  
+   - Add `rel="noopener noreferrer"` and prefetch hints (`<link rel="prefetch">`) for the booking domain.  
+   - Add a 3-step mini-form on the `/contact` page (name, email, goal) that submits to `/api/leads` with `source: "contact_form"`.
+
+3. **Lead magnets**  
+   - Gate a PDF resource (e.g., "The 90-Day Growth Forecast Template") behind email capture.  
+   - Deliver via a server action that writes to `leads` and triggers a Resend welcome sequence.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 6: Competitive Intelligence & Positioning (Ongoing)
+
+*Win condition: `vs-others` becomes the highest-converting page on the site; it outranks the #1 organic competitor comparison.*
+
+1. **Scale `/vs-others`**  
+   - Move from static 10-row table to **selectable competitor + industry** combos (e.g., "BAZ vs HubSpot for B2B SaaS", "BAZ vs Meta agencies in MENA").  
+   - Add per-competitor PDF battle cards gated by email.  
+   - Add schema `FAQPage` for every "BAZ vs X" query.
+
+2. **Competitive SEO monitoring**  
+   - Track rankings for branded vs unbranded competitor keywords.  
+   - Publish public "Stance" updates when a major competitor pivots (e.g., changes pricing model, ships a new product).
+
+3. **Backlink & PR engine**  
+   - 1 "data study" per quarter from anonymized `clients` data (e.g., "CAC benchmarks by channel, H1 2026, 60 brands").  
+   - Pitch 1 guest post per month to relevant trade pubs (e.g., Digiday, MarketingProfs) with original data.  
+   - Turn case studies into LinkedIn carousels and short-form video scripts.
+
+---
+
+## Success Metrics & Validation
+
+| Metric | Baseline | Target (90 days) | Measurement |
+|--------|----------|------------------|-------------|
+| Security/Quality audit score | 2 issues | 0 Critical/High | Fresh `AUDIT.md` run |
+| Organic traffic | *measure now* | +40% | Vercel Analytics / GA4 |
+| Top-3 rankings | *measure now* | +20 | Ahrefs / Screaming Frog |
+| Core Web Vitals | *measure now* | All "Good" | PageSpeed Insights |
+| Contact conversion rate | *measure now* | >4% | `/api/leads` volume vs sessions |
+| Bounce rate | *measure now* | <45% | Vercel Analytics |
+| Backlinks | *measure now* | +50 referring domains | Ahrefs |
+
+**Validation steps:**
+1. After Phase 1, re-run full audit (`npm run inspect` or equivalent) and `npm run build` from a clean clone.
+2. After Phase 3, run `next build` + Lighthouse CI on staging for 5 representative pages (home, service, case study, industry, insights).
+3. Weekly: rank tracker snapshot and organic KW delta.
+4. Bi-weekly: `git diff --stat` review to ensure no design-system regressions.
+
+---
+
+## Dependencies & Risks
+
+- **Env secrets:** `SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY`, `VERCEL_KV_REST_API_URL`, `VERCEL_KV_REST_API_TOKEN` must exist before KV rate limiting lands.
+- **Signed case studies:** Phase 2 stalls without client approval. If unavailable, publish with explicit "illustrative composite" labels and deprioritize CaseStudy schema until signed.
+- **Booking provider:** Cal.com / TidyCal / custom form decision needed before Phase 5.
+- **Competitor data:** Building dynamic `vs-others` combos requires a maintained competitor dataset (names, pricing, differentiators).
+
+---
+
+## Files to Watch (examples)
+
+- `middleware.ts` — auth bypass risk
+- `lib/rate-limit.ts` — production safety
+- `content/case-studies.ts` — trust authority
+- `content/testimonials.ts` — trust authority
+- `app/page.tsx` — LCP & conversion
+- `app/vs-others/page.tsx` — competitive positioning
+- `lib/seo.ts` — global metadata utilities
+- `app/layout.tsx` — CSS import order, fonts, theme
+- `vercel.json` — caching headers
+- `next.config.mjs` — image formats, headers

+# Digital Marketing Fundamentals
+
+## Definition
+
+Digital marketing is the promotion of products, services, or brands through
+internet-connected channels and technologies — search engines, social media,
+email, websites, and mobile apps. The American Marketing Association defines it
+broadly as "any marketing methods conducted through electronic devices which
+utilize some form of a computer."
+
+The defining difference from traditional marketing is **measurability**: nearly
+every click, open, and purchase is trackable, turning marketing from guesswork
+into a discipline.
+
+## The durable fundamentals (they outlast tactics)
+
+1. **Core channels** — SEO, content, social, email, paid ads, affiliate/influencer.
+2. **The owned / earned / paid media model.**
+3. **A data & measurement loop.**
+4. **The customer funnel applied across digital touchpoints.**
+
+Tools and platforms change; these foundations don't.
+
+## The core channels
+
+| Channel | What it does | Character |
+| --- | --- | --- |
+| SEO | Earn organic visibility when people search | Durable, high-trust, compounds (3–6+ mo) |
+| Content marketing | Attract by being useful, not interruptive | Compounds; fuels other channels |
+| Social media | Build audience, community, brand | Discovery + relationship |
+| Email | Nurture an owned audience directly | Highest ROI (~$36–42 per $1) |
+| Paid ads / PPC | Buy attention & traffic | Fast, controllable; stops with budget |
+| Affiliate / influencer | Reach via partners' borrowed trust | Leverage others' audiences |
+
+## Owned, Earned, Paid media
+
+- **Owned** — your website, blog, email list, app. You control them; they compound.
+- **Earned** — press, shares, reviews, word of mouth. Can't buy directly; most credible.
+- **Paid** — ads across search/social/display. Fast and controllable but rented.
+
+A strong strategy balances all three: paid rents demand, owned builds assets,
+earned grants credibility.
+
+## The data & measurement loop
+
+- Visibility into what works lets you find and fix drop-off points.
+- Modern principle: **"measure what matters, respect the customer, act on the
+  data you can ethically collect"** — not "track everything" (privacy changes,
+  cookie restrictions, regulation).
+- Metrics that matter: CAC (customer acquisition cost), ROAS (return on ad
+  spend), CPL (cost per lead), CLV/LTV (lifetime value), conversion rate.
+- ROI formula: `(Revenue - Marketing Cost) / Marketing Cost × 100`.
+- Rule of thumb benchmarks cited: aim for ~3:1 ROAS on paid, ~5:1 on organic.
+
+## A starter framework
+
+| Step | Focus | Why |
+| --- | --- | --- |
+| 1 | Fundamentals | Understand channels, media model, data, funnel |
+| 2 | One channel | Build real competence in a single discipline |
+| 3 | A real project | Apply with actual stakes and feedback |
+| 4 | Analytics | Set up measurement to learn what works |
+| 5 | Integration | Add channels and connect them into one system |
+
+## 8-step strategy framework
+
+1. Define business objectives & SMART marketing goals.
+2. Understand your audience deeply.
+3. Audit your current digital presence.
+4. Select your channel mix.
+5. Develop your content strategy.
+6. Set budget and allocate resources.
+7. Implement tracking & attribution.
+8. Launch, measure, and optimize.
+
+## What's changing in 2026 (AI era)
+
+- Discovery is going **visual and conversational**: Google AI Overviews (~16% of
+  queries), ChatGPT search, Perplexity, Gemini, and platform-native search
+  (TikTok, YouTube, Reddit).
+- New optimization layers: **AEO** (Answer Engine Optimization — get cited by AI
+  engines) and **GEO** (Generative Engine Optimization — content shaped for AI
+  synthesis).
+- Privacy-first marketing and first-party data ownership are now essential.
+- The fundamentals stay the same; execution becomes faster, more personalized,
+  more visual.
+
+## Typical result timelines
+
+- Paid (PPC/social ads): leads within 24–48 hours.
+- Social organic: engagement in days, conversion tuning 2–4 weeks.
+- SEO: meaningful movement in 3–6 months, big gains 6–12 months.
+- Email (existing list): immediate.
+
+## Sources
+
+- What is digital marketing? — Adobe: https://business.adobe.com/blog/basics/digital-marketing
+- Digital Marketing Complete Guide (2026) — PxlPeak: https://pxlpeak.com/guide/digital-marketing-complete-guide
+- Fundamentals of Digital Marketing (2026) — BrandClickX: https://brandclickx.com/fundamentals-of-digital-marketing/
+- Digital Marketing: The Complete 2026 Guide — Frostbite: https://frostbitemarketing.com/resources/guides/digital-marketing-guide-2026/
+- What Is Digital Marketing? A Complete 2026 Guide — Three Girls Media: https://threegirlsmedia.co.uk/digital-marketing-explained-a-complete-2026-guide/
+- Ultimate Guide to Digital Marketing (PDF) — DigitalMarketer: https://www.digitalmarketer.com/digital-marketing/assets/pdf/ultimate-guide-to-digital-marketing.pdf

+# Marketing Documentation
+
+A curated set of marketing reference docs compiled from authoritative sources on
+the web. Each file summarizes key concepts and best practices, then lists the
+source URLs so you can go deeper.
+
+> Note: Snippets were gathered via web search. Treat these docs as a starting
+> reference; always verify platform-specific rules against the primary source,
+> as they change frequently.
+
+## Contents
+
+| File | Topic |
+| --- | --- |
+| [01-digital-marketing-fundamentals.md](01-digital-marketing-fundamentals.md) | Core channels, media models, funnel, measurement |
+| [02-seo.md](02-seo.md) | Search engine optimization (Google-first) |
+| [03-email-marketing.md](03-email-marketing.md) | Deliverability, authentication, list health |
+| [04-social-media-marketing.md](04-social-media-marketing.md) | Platform strategy, content, engagement |
+| [05-paid-ads-ppc.md](05-paid-ads-ppc.md) | Google Ads structure & campaign best practices |
+| [06-content-marketing.md](06-content-marketing.md) | Strategy framework, production, distribution |
+
+## How the channels fit together
+
+The dominant theme across every source: **integration**. No single channel wins
+alone. A strong system looks like this:
+
+- **Owned media** (site, blog, email list) — assets you control and that compound.
+- **Earned media** (press, shares, reviews, word of mouth) — highest credibility.
+- **Paid media** (search/social/display ads) — fast, controllable, stops when budget stops.
+
+SEO feeds content, content fuels social and email, and paid amplifies the best of it.
+
+## The customer funnel (applied digitally)
+
+1. **Awareness** — SEO, social, paid ads.
+2. **Consideration** — content, email, reviews.
+3. **Conversion** — landing pages, offers, checkout.
+4. **Retention & loyalty** — email, community, post-purchase experience.
+
+_Last compiled: 2026-07-10_

data.text:
Confirmed diagnosis:
- `pty.node` NEEDED `libnode.so.115`
- The `code-oss` binary does NOT link libnode (it's statically built, Electron bundles node internally with no external libnode.so, OR node is inside the binary). 
- There is NO libnode.so.115 anywhere on the system.

So the node-pty module was built against Node 20 ABI 115, but the electron 34 binary doesn't expose libnode.so.115 as an external shared object. In a proper electron build, electron ships `libnode.so.115` (because electron builds node as a shared library named libnode.so with SONAME libnode.so.<abi>). But here the build has node statically linked into code-oss (no external libnode). Meanwhile node-pty was built expecting the shared libnode.

This is a genuine packaging bug in this code-oss build. The node-pty pty.node expects libnode.so.115 to be available as a shared library in the library search path, but electron here doesn't provide it.

Fix options:
1. **Provide libnode.so.115** so pty.node can load. We need a libnode.so.115 that is ABI-compatible with electron 34's internal node. But electron's node is statically linked; pty.node loaded by electron will use electron's node runtime. The pty.node only needs libnode.so.115 so the dynamic loader is satisfied — but actually it needs the symbols from node (NAPI, V8). If we provide a libnode.so.115 that has the needed symbols but is a different build, it may conflict. Actually node-pty uses N-API (node_api.h) which is stable, so it just needs the N-API symbols exported by libnode. If we point libnode.so.115 to a real node 20 libnode.so.115, pty.node would resolve N-API symbols from it — but then there'd be two node instances? No — electron loads pty.node into electron's process which already has node. The pty.node's DT_NEEDED libnode.so.115 must resolve to SOME file. If we provide a libnode.so.115 that exports the N-API symbols, the loader resolves them from that file, and pty.node calls into N-API which is implemented by... hmm, actually N-API symbols live in libnode. If electron statically links node, the N-API symbols are already in the main executable. The pty.node's needed libnode.so.115 is just for symbol resolution; if a libnode.so.115 exists with those symbols, loader binds them — but calls would go to the separate libnode, not electron's internal node. That would break.

This is getting really deep and risky. The cleanest, correct fix: **rebuild node-pty's pty.node against the electron 34 headers** so it doesn't need an external libnode, OR rebuild it as a N-API module that's ABI-independent. But that requires node-gyp + electron headers + the exact node version.

Alternatively, the simplest robust solution: **install a libnode.so.115** that is the node 20 shared library, and set LD_LIBRARY_PATH so pty.node loads. But as discussed, the double-node problem.

Hold on. Let me reconsider. Actually, many code-oss / VSCodium builds DO ship libnode.so.115 alongside code-oss, because electron is built with node as shared lib. The fact this build lacks it is the bug. 

The most reliable path: get the matching libnode.so.115. Electron 34's node ABI is 127, not 115. Wait — the pty.node says 115. So whoever built this bundled a node-pty built for node 20 (115) but electron 34 (node 22, ABI 127). That's the real inconsistency.

So even if we provide libnode.so.115 (node 20), pty.node built for ABI 115 would still be loaded into node 22 (electron 34) runtime. NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch → electron's module loader would refuse to load it (it checks process.versions.modules). Actually pty.node is loaded via require of a .node addon; node checks the ABI version embedded in the addon against the running process. If mismatch, it throws "The module was compiled against a different Node.js version". 

But wait, the error we got was a dlopen failure of libnode.so.115 at the dynamic-linker level (process.func dlopen), which happens BEFORE node's ABI check, because the DT_NEEDED can't be resolved. So the linker fails first.

So there are two issues stacked. Even providing libnode.so.115, the ABI check would fail because 115 ≠ 127.

Therefore the ONLY correct fix is to rebuild node-pty against electron 34's node (ABI 127). 

Let me check: does the system have node + node-gyp? Can we install electron headers? This could be heavy. Alternatively, there might be a prebuilt pty.node for electron 34 available via npm `@vscode/node-pty` or `@atom/...`. Actually VS Code's node-pty is `@vscode/node-pty-prebuilt-multiplatform`. Hmm.

Let me step back and think about what's practical in this environment. Let me check what node/npm is available and whether we can rebuild node-pty with electron-rebuild or node-gyp targeting electron 34.

Actually, a much simpler and very common workaround that actually WORKS: set the environment variable to make electron use a shared libnode. But there's none.

Let me reconsider whether maybe a newer code-oss version fixes this. But we can't easily reinstall.

Let me check: is there an npm/node available, and the dev headers? Let me check.

Actually, the most pragmatic robust solution given constraints: rebuild node-pty for electron 34 ABI using `electron-rebuild` or `node-gyp` with `--runtime=electron --target=34.3.2`. Let me see if we can.

But this requires the electron 34 headers and a matching node-gyp. The pty.node source is in resources/app/node_modules/node-pty. node-pty has a binding.gyp and src. We'd need python, make, g++, node headers for electron.

This is a big undertaking but doable. Let me check tooling availability first.

Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the ABI numbers. Node ABI table:
- Node 18 = 108
- Node 20 = 115
- Node 21 = 120
- Node 22 = 127
- Node 23 = 131
- Node 24 = 137 (this matches system libnode.so.137!)

So system node is Node 24 (ABI 137). Electron 34 ships Node 22 (ABI 127). pty.node wants 115 (Node 20).

So the pty.node is built for Node 20, completely wrong for electron 34 (Node 22). Definitely needs rebuild.

Let me check available toolchain.

Given the complexity, let me inform the user of the root cause clearly and then attempt the rebuild. But rebuild could be long. Let me probe the environment for node, npm, python, g++, and internet access.

Let me also reconsider: maybe there's an easier fix. VS Code OSS sometimes allows disabling the integrated terminal pty and using the "web" terminal, but that's not practical.

Actually there's another angle: The error at startup `code-oss --version` hanging and printing libnode error suggests the libnode load failure is severe — it happens during main process load, not just terminal. Wait, why would node-pty be loaded at startup for --version? Possibly the main process loads it for the terminal service registration. The hang is concerning.

Let me check if there's a way to run code-oss at all. If the main process can't even start due to this, the whole editor is broken, not just the terminal. But the user said "terminal is not loading" implying the editor loads but terminal panel doesn't. The --version hang might be a different quirk (X11/display). Let me not over-focus on --version.

Plan:
1. Confirm root cause (done): pty.node compiled for wrong Node ABI (115) and missing libnode.so.115.
2. Rebuild node-pty for electron 34 (ABI 127) using node-gyp targeting electron.
3. Replace pty.node.

data.text:
Fixed. The terminal failed because `pty.node` (the native binding behind VS Code's integrated terminal) was built against **Node 20 (ABI 115)** and linked to a missing `libnode.so.115`, while this build bundles **Electron 34 (Node 22, ABI 127)** with no external `libnode`. The dynamic loader couldn't resolve it, so the terminal process never spawned.

What I did:
- Rebuilt `node-pty` from its bundled source against **Node 22 / ABI 127** (matching Electron 34) using clang + node-gyp.
- The new `pty.node` has **no `libnode` dependency** — Node/N-API symbols resolve at load from the `code-oss` binary, which exports 145 `napi_*` symbols.
- Installed it to `/usr/lib/code-oss/resources/app/node_modules/node-pty/build/Release/pty.node` (original backed up at `/tmp/pty.node.bak.115`).
- Verified it loads cleanly under Node 22, exposing `fork/open/resize/process`.

To finish:
1. **Quit code-oss completely** (the old module is cached in the running process).
2. Relaunch it and open a terminal (`Ctrl+\`` or **Terminal → New Terminal**).

If it still doesn't open, check **Help → Toggle Developer Tools → Console** for errors and share them.

set -u
APPDIR=/usr/lib/code-oss/resources/app
EXTDIR=/usr/lib/code-oss/resources/app/extensions
BUILD=/tmp/build_native
rm -rf "$BUILD"; mkdir -p "$BUILD"
export CC=clang CXX=clang++

build_one() {
  local src="$1" name="$2"
  echo "===== BUILD $name ====="
  if [ ! -d "$src" ]; then echo "  MISSING src $src"; return 1; fi
  local d="$BUILD/$name"
  rm -rf "$d"; cp -r "$src" "$d" || { echo "  copy failed"; return 1; }
  cd "$d"
  # ensure node-addon-api & nan available for gyp require()
  npm install --no-save --silent node-addon-api nan >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  if [ ! -f binding.gyp ]; then echo "  no binding.gyp"; return 1; fi
  if npx --yes node-gyp@latest rebuild >/tmp/${name}.log 2>&1; then
    echo "  BUILD OK"
    # locate produced .node files
    find build/Release -name "*.node" -maxdepth 1
    return 0
  else
    echo "  BUILD FAILED (tail):"
    tail -8 /tmp/${name}.log
    return 1
  fi
}

build_one "$APPDIR/node_modules/kerberos" kerberos
build_one "$APPDIR/node_modules/@vscode/spdlog" spdlog
build_one "$APPDIR/node_modules/@vscode/sqlite3" sqlite3
build_one "$APPDIR/node_modules/native-keymap" native-keymap
build_one "$APPDIR/node_modules/native-watchdog" native-watchdog
build_one "$APPDIR/node_modules/@parcel/watcher" parcel-watcher-app
build_one "$EXTDIR/node_modules/@parcel/watcher" parcel-watcher-ext
echo "ALL DONE"
